scenario: Pinnacle SaaS, a publisher running 14 microservices on Camunda 8.8, plans a browser-based portal for 3500 users per day plus a Node.js batch worker. The architect must decide whether the same @camunda8/sdk dependency can run inside the browser bundle or whether a different documented package is required, because the official prerequisites describe a strict runtime constraint for the TypeScript SDK.
question: Which runtime constraint does the Prerequisites table place on the @camunda8/sdk TypeScript SDK?

options.text: The SDK can run in both Node.js and modern web browsers once polyfills for fetch and stream APIs are bundled by the build pipeline of the publisher portal
The SDK runs in any JavaScript runtime that supports ES2020 features, so the publisher can reuse it inside the browser bundle as long as ESM imports are configured properly
The SDK runs in Node.js and cannot run in a web browser due to technical limitations, so a browser portal must use the @camunda8/orchestration-cluster-api package instead
The SDK runs only in Deno today and is mirrored to Node.js via a community polyfill, so the publisher portal can choose either runtime for the worker and the browser tier

a.text: Incorrect. The prerequisite explicitly forbids browser use due to technical limitations; polyfills do not lift this constraint per the docs.

b.text: Incorrect. The page does not list browser support as conditional on ESM or ES2020; it states the SDK cannot run in a web browser at all.

c.text: Correct. The Prerequisites table states the SDK runs in Node.js and cannot run in a web browser due to technical limitations, and points browser projects at @camunda8/orchestration-cluster-api.

d.text: Incorrect. The page lists Node.js as the runtime; Deno is not the documented target for @camunda8/sdk and there is no described community Node.js polyfill.

explanation: The Prerequisites table for the TypeScript SDK lists Node.js as the runtime and notes that the SDK cannot run in a web browser due to technical limitations. Browser applications must use @camunda8/orchestration-cluster-api instead.
